definition: A Jupyter notebook is an interactive document made of ordered cells (code or Markdown); a kernel is the language process that runs the code cells and holds the session's in-memory state (variables, imports) across executions.
layman: A notebook is a page where you mix runnable code, its results, and notes. Behind it sits a 'brain' (the kernel) that remembers everything you've run so far in that session.
content:
## Cells & kernels

### Cells

A notebook is a vertical list of **cells**:

- **Code cells** run in the kernel and show their output beneath them. The prompt `In [n]:` records the *order* a cell was executed (not its position on the page); the last expression is echoed as `Out[n]:`.
- **Markdown cells** render formatted text, headings, math and images for explanation.

Run a cell with **Shift+Enter** (run and move on) or **Ctrl/Cmd+Enter** (run in place).

### Kernels

The **kernel** is the process that executes your code. The crucial idea: **state persists across cells for the whole session.** A variable defined in one cell is visible in any cell you run afterward — regardless of where that cell sits on the page.

Kernel actions you'll use constantly:

| Action | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| Interrupt | stop a running cell (e.g. an infinite loop) |
| Restart | wipe all in-memory state and start fresh |
| Restart & Run All | the gold standard for checking reproducibility top-to-bottom |

### The big gotcha: hidden state

Because state survives and you can run cells in any order, a notebook can *look* correct while depending on a variable from a cell you've since edited or deleted. Always finish with **Restart & Run All** to confirm it works from a clean kernel.

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Notebooks are the default surface for prototyping prompts, calling model APIs, inspecting embeddings and plotting evals. The persistent kernel lets you keep an expensive resource alive — a loaded model, an API client, a cached dataset — and iterate on prompts in later cells without re-running setup each time.
keyPoints: A notebook is ordered cells; code cells execute, Markdown cells document.
The kernel runs code and keeps session state (variables/imports) across cells.
In [n] shows execution order, not on-page position; Out[n] echoes the last expression.
Out-of-order execution causes hidden-state bugs — use Restart & Run All to verify.
A persistent kernel lets you keep models/clients/data loaded while iterating.
